findings: Test 1 (no auth, no idem key) returned 401 auth_required as expected
Test 2 (fake bearer flk_test_fake, no idem key) returned 401 auth_required as expected - auth wins even with malformed token
Test 3 (no auth, with Idempotency-Key: 01HTEST) returned 401 auth_required as expected - auth precedes idempotency validation
All 3 auth_required responses carried RFC7807 type URI https://flowlink.ink/errors/auth_required, code=auth_required, status=401, agent_action pointing to /.well-known/flowlink.md for SIWE flow
Compliance endpoint is unauthenticated (as claimed); Tornado Cash address 0x8589...DA16 correctly returned 403 compliance_blocked_sanctions on all 4 calls
Semantic determinism holds: blocked status, score=0, and detail text identical across all compliance calls
Idempotency-Key appears NOT to replay stored response on compliance endpoint; each call returns a fresh request_id and recomputes

No 409 idempotency_conflict was triggered when reusing key 01HCOMP01 with a different address body - instead upstream returned 503 compliance_upstream_unavailable, suggesting the Idempotency-Key middleware is not enforced on the compliance path
bugs_discovered: DETERMINISM LEAK: first compliance call returns sanctions_source='fallback'; subsequent calls return sanctions_source='cache'. The response body is NOT byte-identical across replays. This is an internal implementation detail leaking into the public response. Either mask sanctions_source from API output, or normalize so replay via Idempotency-Key replays the original 'fallback' token instead of reflecting post-cache-warm state.

IDEMPOTENCY NOT ENFORCED ON COMPLIANCE: same Idempotency-Key with a different request body did NOT return 409 idempotency_conflict as the spec claims. The compliance endpoint appears to bypass the Idempotency-Key middleware entirely. If the 24h dedup guarantee is meant to apply to all writes, /v1/compliance/check should either (a) honor it and emit 409 on body mismatch, or (b) be documented as exempt.
